design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New topic: inference for nonparametric partial-identification bound intersections over rich or continuous covariate level sets. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Manski nonparametric bounds, Imbens-Manski confidence intervals, Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer set inference, and Andrews-Shi moment-inequality inference. Our theorem is not that because the object is the nonregular endpoint functional formed by intersecting estimated level-set bounds as the active level-set geometry changes. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a tangent-cone or EIF-style phase threshold separating unique-active, finite-multi-active, and continuum-active flat-face regimes, plus a hand-derived bootstrap-failure or limit-law witness. If the proposal reduces to ordinary interval intersection, generic moment inequalities, or finite-cell Manski bounds, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New topic: inference for nonparametric partial-identification bound intersections over rich or continuous covariate level sets. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Manski nonparametric bounds, Imbens-Manski confidence intervals, Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer set inference, and Andrews-Shi moment-inequality inference. Our theorem is not that because the object is the nonregular endpoint functional formed by intersecting estimated level-set bounds as the active level-set geometry changes. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a tangent-cone or EIF-style phase threshold separating unique-active, finite-multi-active, and continuum-active flat-face regimes, plus a hand-derived bootstrap-failure or limit-law witness. If the proposal reduces to ordinary interval intersection, generic moment inequalities, or finite-cell Manski bounds, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue item is `manski_nonparametric_bounds_v1` / API Sec. 3.1 `t3_thm`, whose headline statement intersects pointwise Manski intervals over covariate sets. The proposal does not restate that bound formula; it takes the lower endpoint produced by such an intersection as the target and studies the sampling and bootstrap law when two covariate levels are locally tied. The adjacent banked proposals flag continuous-covariate regularization and centered-bootstrap undercoverage, but none states the random contact-gap law for `L(P)=max_x ell_P(x)`.","published_axis":"ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 is the closest intersection-bound paper, FangSantos2019 is the closest nonsmooth-bootstrap paper, and ImbensManski2004 is the closest scalar partial-ID interval-inference anchor. The gap is that none of these papers hand-derives the two-level Manski-style bound-intersection endpoint where the centered bootstrap conditions on the random observed active gap, yielding the proposed failure witness."}
seed_list: Develop a contact-cone derivative for L(P)=sup_x ell_P(x) separating singleton, finite-tie, and continuum-flat covariate contact regimes.
Construct a two-point bound-intersection witness where the centered naive bootstrap has a random conditional law at a tie.
Build an adaptive enlarged-contact-set confidence procedure for continuum-active endpoints that is less conservative than generic CMI sets.
Give a finite-face versus infinite-face reduction theorem bridging calibrated projection and continuum covariate-contact endpoints.
Define a critical contact-gap threshold Delta_n(P) governing when near-active covariates enter the endpoint limit law.
Give a support-function representation of the continuum-active endpoint derivative as a non-LP sharp partial-ID inference object.
Derive the upper-endpoint analog for U(P)=inf_x u_P(x) and show simultaneous lower-upper endpoint inference fails under opposing contact phases.
Construct a measurable-selection theorem for Borel-support bound intersections sufficient for contact-set bootstrap inference.
Characterize when finite-cell Manski intersections approximate continuous-support endpoint laws without changing the limiting contact phase.
Give a local-drift experiment showing plug-in percentile intervals undercover when active contact gaps are n^{-1/2}-local.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a contact-cone derivative for L(P)=sup_x ell_P(x) separating singleton, finite-tie, and continuum-flat covariate contact regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. Rosen; Zheng Fang, Andres Santos
anchor_paper.claim: Intersection bounds and directional delta methods cover suprema and nonsmooth maps but not the endpoint contact-phase diagram.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: adaptive inequality/moment selection methods; standard bootstrap may fail

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends intersection-bound inference; stronger because active-contact geometry creates distinct finite and continuum limit laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 plus FangSantos2019 because L(P) is just a supremum functional.
seed_details.rebuttal: The natural attempt fails because the active covariate contact set moves with the local law; the seed names the phase object missing from both anchors.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 because it characterizes the tangent contact set and phase-specific endpoint law, not adaptive selection for a generic supremum.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine supremum calculus is used to create a new contact-cone phase object and a finite-versus-continuum threshold.

adversarial_referee.instance: The focal object could look like a renamed argmax set.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by defining Phi_P(h) and computing it in Exhibit 9.1 from bound primitives.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only sufficient regularity for a derivative may be stated.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal phrases necessity only as phase separation, not as a full iff identification condition.

adversarial_referee.instance: CLR may already cover contact-set selection implicitly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: CLR covers adaptive inequality selection, but not singleton/finite/continuum endpoint phase classification.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a two-point bound-intersection witness where the centered naive bootstrap has a random conditional law at a tie.

anchor_paper.author: Zheng Fang, Andres Santos; Guido Imbens, Charles Manski
anchor_paper.claim: The general bootstrap failure theorem and scalar interval nonuniformity do not give a compact partial-ID contact-gap witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: standard bootstrap may fail; do not converge uniformly

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/search; stronger because the witness is non-degenerate with two active observable covariate atoms.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the textbook max(a,b) bootstrap failure example.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed embeds max(a,b) as an identified-set endpoint generated by intersected covariate bounds and computes the bootstrap face drift.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not FangSantos2019 because it supplies a partial-ID endpoint witness tied to bound intersections rather than a generic nonsmooth map.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the routine max example becomes a named comparator-divergence DGP for intersection-bound inference.

adversarial_referee.instance: Two covariate points may be too hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by nonzero Gaussian variation in both cells and explicit extension path to finite ties.
Partly lands; keep as Theorem witness supporting the broader Conjectures, not the sole flagship.
The claim is not general failure novelty; it is the hand-derived bound-intersection witness requested by the gap.

seed_details.one_line: Build an adaptive enlarged-contact-set confidence procedure for continuum-active endpoints that is less conservative than generic CMI sets.

anchor_paper.author: Donald Andrews, Xiaoxia Shi; Sokbae Lee, Kyungchul Song, Yoon-Jae Whang
anchor_paper.claim: CMI and functional-inequality inference use generalized selection or contact sets but not endpoint-specific continuum contact inference.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: generalized moment selection; estimating contact sets

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends CMI to endpoint inference; stronger only if the contact enlargement yields a named coverage theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an implementation variant of AndrewsShi2013 or LeeSongWhang2018.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap payload says off-the-shelf CMI bands do not target a sharp endpoint law over an estimated Borel contact set.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not AndrewsShi2013 because it calibrates to the endpoint contact set rather than all conditional inequalities.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless paired with the contact-cone theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite-face versus infinite-face reduction theorem bridging calibrated projection and continuum covariate-contact endpoints.

anchor_paper.author: Hiroaki Kaido, Francesca Molinari, Joerg Stoye; Arie Beresteanu, Francesca Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-dimensional projection and support-function inference do not decide when a covariate contact set stays infinite-dimensional.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge; stronger because it asks for a finite-vs-infinite reduction criterion, not another projection interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is ordinary support-function inference for a convex set face.
seed_details.rebuttal: The active face here is indexed by covariates and smoothing topology, not a finite moment-inequality polyhedron.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not KaidoMolinariStoye2019 because the theorem classifies when the active program is not finite-dimensional.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the reduction criterion is expressed in observable contact topology; otherwise collapses to field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: The threshold between finite and infinite faces may be vague.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by requiring a contact-set cardinality/topology criterion.

adversarial_referee.instance: KMS targets projections, not endpoints of functions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal uses KMS only as finite-face comparator, not as same target.

adversarial_referee.instance: Reduction may prove only finite contact suffices.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Converse would require a countercondition yielding Gaussian supremum; keep as conjectural.

seed_details.one_line: Define a critical contact-gap threshold Delta_n(P) governing when near-active covariates enter the endpoint limit law.

anchor_paper.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. Rosen; Timothy Armstrong
anchor_paper.claim: Intersection-bound and CMI rate theory do not supply an endpoint-specific near-contact threshold.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: potentially infinite constraint set; rate of convergence

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; stronger because it names a phase threshold rather than broadening assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just local alternatives for moment inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: The threshold is tied to the endpoint contact gap L(P)-ell_P(x), not slackness of every inequality in a CMI test.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Armstrong2014 because it is an endpoint contact-gap scalar, not a weighted KS critical-value rate result.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if Delta_n(P) is computed from observable bound maps and yields phase changes.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical threshold may be free epsilon.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by tying it to n^{1/2} contact gaps defined from ell_P.

adversarial_referee.instance: May state only a sufficient near-contact condition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Keep as threshold conjecture with boundary drift penalty.

adversarial_referee.instance: CMI local power already handles slackness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The endpoint threshold uses argmax contact, not all-inequality slackness.

seed_details.one_line: Give a support-function representation of the continuum-active endpoint derivative as a non-LP sharp partial-ID inference object.

anchor_paper.author: Arie Beresteanu, Francesca Molinari; Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Support-function and intersection-bound papers do not represent the continuum-active endpoint derivative as a contact-set support functional.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Non-LP sharp representation; stronger because the focal object is a Gaussian support functional over C_L(P).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a relabeling of support functions for random closed sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: The support function is over the tangent process restricted to covariate contacts, not over the identified set itself.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not BeresteanuMolinari2008 because the represented object is the endpoint derivative, not the population identification region.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: creates a new evaluable contact-support functional.

adversarial_referee.instance: Support function may be too abstract to evaluate.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit 9.1 computes it for two atoms; continuum evaluation route uses Gaussian process restriction.
Upper endpoint follows by -u_P; noted in scope.

adversarial_referee.instance: BM may already cover support functions generally.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: BM support functions identify sets; this derivative governs an intersection endpoint law.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the upper-endpoint analog for U(P)=inf_x u_P(x) and show simultaneous lower-upper endpoint inference fails under opposing contact phases.

anchor_paper.author: Guido Imbens, Charles Manski; Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Scalar interval inference and two-sided intersection bounds do not classify opposing lower/upper contact phases.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: lower and upper bounds; two-sided bound estimates

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation; stronger if opposing phases create nonrectangular joint endpoint law.
seed_details.referee_objection: Upper endpoint is mechanically obtained by applying the lower result to -u.
seed_details.rebuttal: The individual endpoint is mechanical, but simultaneous coverage can fail when lower and upper contacts have different local phases.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless joint lower-upper law is central.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a measurable-selection theorem for Borel-support bound intersections sufficient for contact-set bootstrap inference.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Rich Borel-support extrema raised measurable-selection and simultaneous-band obstacles.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: whether the essential-envelope sharpness argument for arbitrary Borel supports needs a measurable-selection lemma

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends rich-support extrema; weaker because it risks becoming technical regularity rather than a theorem-level contribution.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a measurability patch, not a publishable econometric kernel.
seed_details.rebuttal: True for a standalone seed; it is useful only as a support lemma for continuum-contact inference.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize when finite-cell Manski intersections approximate continuous-support endpoint laws without changing the limiting contact phase.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith catalogue and prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Existing catalogue intersects pointwise Manski intervals across covariate sets but does not treat continuous-contact inference.
anchor_paper.location: doc/API.md Section 3.1; RDD proposal Honest scope flags
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: continuous covariates would require an additional measurability and regularization layer

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Finite-to-continuous extension; stronger only if approximation preserves or changes the phase law at a sharp boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is numerical discretization of a supremum.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial issue is whether discretization changes the active-contact phase and bootstrap law.

seed_details.one_line: Give a local-drift experiment showing plug-in percentile intervals undercover when active contact gaps are n^{-1/2}-local.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal; Fang and Santos
anchor_paper.venue: CausalSmith bank / Review of Economic Studies
anchor_paper.claim: Prior bootstrap undercoverage witness and Fang-Santos theory suggest local drift can break centered resampling.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: coverage loss; standard bootstrap may fail

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/search; stronger if the local drift is an open class rather than one tuned sequence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats a prior DML bootstrap witness with a different label.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed changes the object to partial-ID bound intersections and active contact gaps, not orthogonality frontiers.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 because the drift is a covariate contact gap in an endpoint functional.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if promoted from witness to local experiment class; otherwise field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be a single tuned local sequence.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need an open local-drift class; not chosen as v1 kernel.
Use as applied witness, not general theorem novelty.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack focal object beyond undercoverage.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object would be n^{1/2} contact gap; otherwise downgrade.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 literature map used as substrate. Manski (1990, AER, Manski1990) supplies bounded-outcome partial-ID bounds, not moving-contact endpoint inference. Manski and Pepper (2000, Econometrica, ManskiPepper2000) sharpen bounds with covariate-indexed monotone IV inequalities. Imbens and Manski (2004, Econometrica, ImbensManski2004) solve scalar interval inference and note nonuniformity as interval width changes. Chernozhukov, Hong, and Tamer (2007, Econometrica, ChernozhukovHongTamer2007) give broad moment-inequality set inference. Beresteanu and Molinari (2008, Econometrica, BeresteanuMolinari2008) give support-function inference for partially identified sets. Andrews and Shi (2013, Econometrica, AndrewsShi2013) give conditional-moment-inequality inference with generalized moment selection. Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica, ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013) is the closest direct anchor on intersection bounds. Armstrong (2014, JoE, Armstrong2014) shows rate and critical-value changes for weighted KS CMI inference. Lee, Song, and Whang (2018, Econometric Theory, LeeSongWhang2018) use estimated contact sets for functional inequality tests. Kaido, Molinari, and Stoye (2019, Econometrica, KaidoMolinariStoye2019) handle finite-dimensional calibrated projection. Fang and Santos (2019, ReStud, FangSantos2019) supply directional-differentiability and bootstrap-failure theory. Remaining gap: endpoint inference for level-set bound intersections whose active geometry changes among singleton, finite-tie, and continuum flat-face regimes.
